steps.title: Identify the sides relative to angle Q
steps.content:
In right triangle $QRS$, the side opposite angle $Q$ is $\overline{RS}$ (length $17$). Since $QR < QS$, $\overline{QS}$ is the hypotenuse.
$$\sin Q = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \frac{RS}{QS} = \frac{17}{QS}$$

steps.title: Solve for QS
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $QS$ and divide by $\sin Q$:
$$QS = \frac{17}{\sin Q}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
